steps.title: Identify future visualization
steps.content: The claim specifies that Newland “visualizes the details of a future with May.” The key signal is active imagining or picturing of a future scenario—not present observation.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content: Choice D explicitly states Newland's “imagination...pictured [May] at his side,” with his thoughts leaping ahead to the engagement ring, betrothal kiss, and wedding march from Lohengrin. The words “imagination” and “pictured” directly express visualization of a future with May. The other three show only present observation at the opera or describe the opera house. This matches choice D — D is correct.
